nodes.label: File / Document Icon
nodes.description: A small 16x16 document icon showing a page with a folded top-right corner (dog-ear) and three horizontal lines representing text content. Generic file/document symbol filled in gray (#666).

nodes.label: Globe / World / Internet Icon
nodes.description: A 16x16 globe icon depicting a circle overlaid with latitude and longitude grid lines, representing the internet, internationalization (i18n), or a web link. Filled in gray (#666).

nodes.label: Next.js Wordmark Logo
nodes.description: The official Next.js wordmark SVG (394x80 viewBox). Renders the text 'NEXT.JS' in the framework's canonical black logotype. Used as a brand asset identifying the Next.js React framework.

nodes.label: Vercel Logo (Triangle Logomark)
nodes.description: The Vercel logomark: a solid white upward-pointing equilateral triangle on a transparent background (1155x1000 viewBox). Represents the Vercel cloud deployment platform, the default hosting provider for Next.js apps.

nodes.label: Browser / Application Window Icon
nodes.description: A 16x16 icon of a desktop application or browser window: a rounded rectangle with a title bar area containing three small circles (traffic-light buttons) in the top-left corner. Filled in gray (#666). Represents a GUI window or browser tab.

edges.note: Next.js is developed and maintained by Vercel; both logos are default assets scaffolded by create-next-app and co-appear on the default Next.js landing page.
Both icons are part of the default Next.js create-next-app public asset set, used together as decorative link icons on the default landing page (globe = 'learn', window = 'examples').
Both icons are part of the default Next.js create-next-app public asset set, co-scaffolded alongside globe.svg.
Both icons are part of the default Next.js create-next-app public asset set.
